ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-27-2026 Continued To Aug-11-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Lim appeared for and with Rupert McClendon and Stanton McClendon (objectors).
Tenesha Jackson appeared via zoom for Belinda Arterberry (petitioner).

Discussion was held regarding the declaratory relief and void of sale of real property, that parties are open to mediation, and that if things don't resolve at mediation parties are seeking to go to trial.
The Court denied the Petition for Declaratory Relief and Void of Sale of Real Property.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than July 22, 2026. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by July 29,2026. The matter is continued to August 11, 2026 and the Parties are to return on August 11, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Aug-20-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Tenesha Jackson for Administrator Belinda Arterberry (via Zoom).
Jennifer Lim for Beneficiaries Rupert McClendon, Vera Anderson, and Stanton McClendon (via Zoom).
Rupert McClendon was present in the courtroom.

The matter came on for hearing on: 1) status on filing of receipts for blocked accounts; 2) status on close of estate; and 3) Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause why attorneys should not be sanctioned for failure to appear.

The Court addressed the order to show cause first. Ms. Jackson admitted she mistakenly believed her filing of the declaration regarding blocked accounts resolved the matter and failed to submit a status report or accounting until the day before the hearing. Ms. Lim explained she mistakenly calendared the hearing for 10:00 a.m., appeared late by Zoom, and missed the call of the case. The Court found that calendaring errors were not good cause and imposed sanctions of $250 against each attorney.

Regarding the status of closing the estate, the Court noted a petition had recently been filed with a hearing set for October 28, 2025, and took that status matter off calendar.

On the receipts for blocked accounts, Ms. Jackson reported that her client had repeatedly attempted to obtain receipts from Chase and BMO, but the banks required processing through their legal departments and provided no direct contact. A copy confirming the Wells Fargo account was submitted, and Ms. Jackson confirmed all other funds, except for an operating account containing approximately $19,000, were in blocked accounts. The Court took the hearing on status of filing receipts for blocked accounts off calendar, based on counsel's representation.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Jul-23-2025 Continued To Aug-20-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Stanton and Rupert McClendon appeared in person. (beneficiaries).

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Tenesha Jackson (attorney) to appear on August 20, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against her for failure to appear. Status on Filing of Receipts for Blocked Account and Status on Close of Estate are continued to that date.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Jennifer Lim (attorney) to appear on August 20, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against her for failure to appear. Status on Filing of Receipts for Blocked Account and Status on Close of Estate are continued to that date.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Ng. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipts For The Blocked Accounts
Off Calendar Jul-23-2025 Continued To Aug-20-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Continued To Jul-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Tenesha Jackson for Belinda Arterberry, Administrator (via Zoom).
Jennifer Lim for Rupert McClendon, Veda Anderson, and Stanton McClendon, Beneficiaries (via Zoom).
Rupert McClendon, Veda Anderson, and Stanton McClendon were present in court.
Tihisha Medina, Benefiary (via Zoom).

Ms. Jackson reported that all blocked account deposits had been made. However, the Administrator was informed by the banks that the acknowledgment and receipt forms would be mailed directly to the court, and the banks refused to release copies to her. The Court indicated no such receipts had been filed and instructed Ms. Jackson to file a declaration that is signed and verified by her client that includes copies of bank statements showing proof of the deposits. Ms. Jackson was further instructed to resend the deposit forms to the banks in an effort to obtain executed receipts. The Court set a compliance deadline of June 11, 2025, for filing the declaration and bank statements.

Regarding status of closing the estate, Ms. Jackson reported that co-counsel, G. Kat Stokes, expects to have the petition for final distribution ready for filing within two weeks.

Ms. Lim raised concerns regarding the administration of the estate. She cited discrepancies between the appraised value of the decedent's property ($770,000) and the sale price ($599,000), as well as information suggesting the buyer obtained a mortgage loan of $701,000, exceeding the purchase price.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to July 23, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipts For The Blocked Accounts
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Continued To Jul-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Feb-19-2025 Continued To May-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Tenesha Jackson for Belinda Arterberry, Administrator (via Zoom).
Jennifer Lim for Rupart McClendon and Veda Anderson, Beneficiaries (via Zoom).
Stanton McClendon, Beneficiary in Pro Per.

Ms. Jackson reported that a Chase account has been opened but Chase has not provided a receipt to her client, stating that it was sent to their legal department. Accounts at Wells Fargo and BMO are still not opened, as the banks required the court order to be on a Judicial Council form. The court instructed Ms. Jackson to email a proposed Judicial Council form of order to the probate examiner. Ms. Jackson stated that once all blocked accounts are opened, she will prepare the petition for final distribution and accounting.

Discussions were held regarding creditor claims from the beneficiaries. Ms. Jackson indicated that the creditor's claims were submitted late and will require petitioning for late claim allowance. Counsel requested a continuance to allow time for addressing creditor's claims and preparations for filing a petition for final distribution.

The court continued the matter to May 28, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Dec-17-2024 Continued To Feb-19-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Gerald Stokes and Tenesha Jackson for Belinda Arterberry, Administrator (via Zoom).
Belinda Arterberry, Administrator is present in court.
Rupart McClendon, Beneficiary in Propria Persona.
Veda Anderson, Beneficiary in Propria Persona.
Stanton McClendon, Beneficiary in Propria Persona.

The court acknowledged Mr. Stokes' and Ms. Jackson's explanations for prior absences at the November 19th hearing and opted not to impose sanctions. The Order to Show Cause/Order to Appear was taken off calendar by the court.

Discussions were held regarding status on filing receipts for blocked accounts. Ms. Jackson explained that inconsistencies in the letters of administration were causing the banks to reject funds, and that she intends to file an ex parte petition to amend the letters.

Ms. Arterberry stated that sale proceeds are currently held as cashier's checks and that she will deposit these funds into blocked accounts upon receipt of updated letters. The beneficiaries expressed their concerns and frustrations with regards to alleged mismanagement of estate funds, payments made for property takex and burial expenses, and the lack of accounting for estate funds. Mr. Stokes indicated the final accounting will address all claims, objections, and creditor issues.

The court continued the status hearings to February 19, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Stanton McClendon, beneficiary, appearing in Propria Persona.
Rupart McClendon, beneficiary, appearing in Propria Persona.
Veda McClendon Anderson, beneficiary, appearing in Propria Persona.

The court notes the absence of attorneys Gerald C. Stokes and Tenesha M. Jackson. The family members express their concerns about being reimbursed for payments made towards property taxes, funeral expenses and construction/labor performed on the property. They each expressed frustration with the lack of communication and trust between them and the administrator.

An Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause will be issued to the administrator Belinda Arterberry to appear on December 17, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why she failed to appear and to show why she should not be removed as the administrator for failure to file receipts for the blocked accounts.

An Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause will be issued to attorney Gerald C. Stokes to appear on December 17, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear and to show why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against him for failure to appear.

An Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause will be issued to attorney Tenesha M. Jackson to appear on December 17, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why she failed to appear and to show why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against her for failure to appear.

Status of filing the receipts for the blocked accounts is also continued to December 17, 2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Off Calendar Nov-05-2024 Continued To Nov-19-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2335 Ralmar Ave., East Palo Alto, Ca 94303)
Off Calendar Oct-16-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gerald Stokes for Belinda Arterberry (administrator).
Jonathan Crossley (listing agent) also present.
Jaime Rodriguez (buyer's agent) also present with Eric Chan (buyer).
Stanton McClendon (heir) in propria persona.

Mr. Stokes confirmed that the brokers agreed to 4% of the commissions to the seller and 1% to the buyer. The sale was confirmed at $599,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property is under submission (subject to review of the order). The proceeds from the sale are to be deposited into blocked accounts at three different FDIC financial institutions in lieu of an additional bond. Status on filing of the receipts for the blocked accounts is set for November 5, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court explained to Mr. McClendon why the re-appraisal amount was lower than the initial appraisal and why the bid in the amount of $599,000.00 meets the requirement of the probate code.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Santos. Not reported.

10:30 a.m.

Reupert McClendon appeared in propria persona after the matter had concluded and presented oral objections, concerns about the lack of administration of the estate and lack of performance by his sister, concerns about being reimbursed for expenses that have been paid by three of the siblings, work that was performed by him, etc. Mr. McClendon was advised to speak with his attorney about bringing an appropriate petition to address his concerns.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-10-2023 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-14-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tanesha Jackson appeared by zoom for Belinda Arterberry.

The court stated that at the previous hearing Rupert McClendon appeared and raised objections, that he was told to submit his objections in writing, that the court has not received any objections, that he is not here, and that the objections are overruled. The Petition for Letters of Administration is under submission with the court's intent to grant. Belinda Arterberry will be appointed as the administrator of the estate with limited authority. Bond in set at $20,000.00. Status on close of estate is set for April 2, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ann Simons is appointed probate referee.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Tan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-31-2023 Continued To Mar-14-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Rupert E. McClendon, son of decedent, in court.

The Court stated that the matter was on for a petition to open a probate which the Court was prepared to grant. In addition, their was a request to appoint Belinda Arterberry as Administrator with a $20,000.00 bond.

Mr. McClendon provided argument in opposition to the petition, requested a 60-day continuance, stated that he consulted with an attorney and would be hiring one, and that he would be filing his own petition for probate. Mr. McClendon also stated that he resided at the residence, that his mother lived there until she got dementia and colon cancer, that he performed maintenance at the residence for the last 15 years, that he paid taxes the last two to three years, that Belinda Arterberry is his sister, and that he objects to her appointment. The Court explainted that Belinda Arterberry is asking to open the probate so that she can administer the estate. Mr. McClendon stated that he will ask to be the administrator. The Court stated that it would grant a 45-day continuance and that it would be looking for Mr. McClendon's petition for probate or his objections to be filed before that date. Mr. McClendon stated that his brother paid for the burial, that his mom had money in an account for her burial, and that his sister mishandled those funds. Mr. McClendon also stated that the house is in Palo Alto, that there are seven siblings, and that one of the siblings is deceased.

At the conclusion of comments, the Petition for Letters Administration is continued to March 14, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Mr. McClendon that it would be looking for either his petition to administer the estate or written objections to be filed prior to March 14, 2023.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Ateesh Datt/Jackie Alameda. Deputy: Pleigo. Not reported.
